title: How Ram Temple donations were received, counted & deposited: Cops quiz Champat Rai
content: Ayodhya police have intensified their probe into alleged embezzlement of Ram Temple donations, recording statements from former Trust general secretary Champat Rai and other functionaries. Investigators are meticulously examining the entire donation management process, from receipt to deposit, to identify procedural lapses and establish accountability. This crucial step aims to reconstruct the movement of funds and uncover any conspiracy, with financial records and evidence being corroborated.

title: Bengal slashes 65 Muslim sub-groups from TMC-era list, clears twin OBC quota bills
content: West Bengal's assembly has passed two bills to revise the OBC reservation framework, now recognizing 66 communities (54 Hindu, 12 Muslim) outside SC/ST categories. This move reverses changes made by the previous TMC government, which had faced legal challenges. The new legislation restores greater power to the West Bengal Commission for Backward Classes, making its recommendations binding on the government for determining reservation percentages and community classifications.

title: Saibari’s winner sends Morocco into last 16 as Netherlands crash to earliest WC exit
content: Morocco stunned the Netherlands in a dramatic penalty shootout, securing a 3-2 victory after a 1-1 draw to advance to the last 16 of the FIFA World Cup. Goalkeeper Yassine Bounou's crucial save paved the way for Ismael Saibari's winning penalty. The Atlas Lions fought back from Cody Gakpo's late goal, forcing extra time with a stoppage-time header, ending the Dutch's World Cup campaign.

title: The Rs 40 lakh house hunt: Which metro gives you the best deal?
content: Finding a home under Rs 40 lakh in India's major cities is becoming a challenge, with only 10% of new launches falling into this category. Kolkata offers the best prospects, boasting 41% of its new supply below this price point. In contrast, cities like NCR and Bengaluru are dominated by luxury projects, making affordable options scarce for budget-conscious buyers.

title: '994 days of genocide': Microsoft engineer quits, emails thousands on his final day
content: A Microsoft data center technician in Italy resigned on his final day, emailing thousands of colleagues across Europe to accuse the company of powering Israel's war in Gaza through its Azure cloud and European data centers. Published by No Azure for Apartheid, the email cites the Unit 8200 surveillance exposé and urges Microsoft workers to "reclaim our labor" and divest.

title: Massive fire breaks out at Bengal's Haldia refinery, several workers injured
content: A significant fire erupted at the Petrochemical Complex in West Bengal early Tuesday morning, engulfing a naphtha-carrying pipeline. Several workers sustained burn injuries and were promptly taken for medical attention. The exact cause of the blaze remains under investigation. Details regarding the full extent of the damage and the condition of those injured are still emerging.

title: Delhi EV policy keeps hybrids out, includes 3-year lock-in period
content: Delhi's new EV policy, effective July 1, prioritizes pure electric vehicles, excluding hybrids. It offers substantial road tax and registration fee exemptions for electric cars under Rs 30 lakh, alongside a Rs 1 lakh incentive for scrapping old cars to buy new EVs. Subsidies are also available for electric two and three-wheelers, aiming to significantly curb pollution and boost clean mobility.

title: India announce Harmanpreet-led squad for Asian Games after T20 World Cup exit
content: India's Women's Selection Committee has unveiled the squad for the 2026 Asian Games in Japan, where they aim to defend their gold medal title. Led by Harmanpreet Kaur, the team features a blend of seasoned stars and emerging talent, including Smriti Mandhana as vice-captain. The squad announcement sets the stage for another strong showing by the Indian women's cricket team on the international stage.

title: Katie Miller is not impressed with Bezos' ex-wife MacKenzie Scott who donated billions
content: MacKenzie Scott's substantial charitable donations, totaling over $26 billion, have drawn criticism from figures like Katie Miller and Elon Musk. Miller specifically pointed to Scott's funding of progressive causes, including Planned Parenthood and racial justice groups.  Scott, who received a significant Amazon stake in her divorce, has pledged to donate the majority of her wealth, supporting diverse non-profits across education, healthcare, and climate initiatives.

title: 'Afraid of Ram devotees coming?' Congress claims leaders under house arrest ahead of Ayodhya visit
content: Ajay Rai, the Congress chief in Uttar Pradesh, has accused the BJP government of placing him under house arrest to stop a party delegation from reaching the revered Ram Temple in Ayodhya. He suggested that the government is anxious about a potential backlash from devotees regarding a donation embezzlement scandal. Rai's wife voiced her concerns, stating that the BJP would be accountable for any harm that might come to him.

title: How 'future prospects' helped a family secure higher accident compensation
content: Andhra Pradesh High Court significantly boosted compensation for a road accident victim's family, raising it from Rs 7.28 lakh to Rs 10.06 lakh. The court found the tribunal underestimated the deceased credit officer's future earnings. Rejecting the APSRTC's claims of contributory negligence, the court emphasized fair compensation, even increasing the award suo motu to account for lost companionship and estate.

title: India’s economy could hit $7 trillion this decade, double by mid-2030s
content: India's economy is projected to reach $7 trillion by 2030, with ambitions for $14 trillion by mid-2030s and $25-30 trillion by 2047, according to Ambassador Vinay Mohan Kwatra.  Meanwhile, the US Ambassador to India, Sergio Gor, expressed optimism about finalizing an interim trade deal soon and hinted at a potential visit by President Trump to India next year, underscoring strengthening bilateral ties.

title: Ex-Google engineer: The one trait Larry Page, Sergey Brin and Sundar Pichai share
content: A former Google employee observed a shared trait among leaders like Sundar Pichai, Larry Page, and Sergey Brin: a complete disregard for conventional thinking.  Pichai's audacious decision to launch Google Chrome, despite widespread skepticism and even mockery, exemplifies this. This 'crazy' thinking, coupled with intense hard work, proved to be the secret formula for success, ultimately leading Chrome to dominate the browser market and paving Pichai's path to CEO.

title: 'Once-in-a-generation player': Latham, Root pay heartfelt tributes to Stokes
content: England's star all-rounder Ben Stokes has bid farewell to Test cricket, announcing his retirement after a memorable career. Despite a loss in his final match against New Zealand, Stokes received glowing tributes from opponents and teammates. New Zealand captain Tom Latham lauded him as a "once-in-a-generation player," while Joe Root reflected on their long-standing friendship and the void Stokes' absence will leave in the dressing room.

title: Apple iPhone 18 Pro data leak: Tata Electronics breach exposes key components
content: A major ransomware attack on Tata Electronics has exposed sensitive Apple supply chain data, including details of upcoming iPhone 18 Pro models and component suppliers, on the dark web. This breach, attributed to the World Leaks group, could significantly impact Apple's global manufacturing strategy and its growing partnership with Tata in India. Apple is reportedly investigating the incident.

title: Erling Haaland, Isabel Haugseng Johansen's combined net worth in 2026
content: Erling Haaland and Isabel Haugseng Johansen have built one of football's most talked-about partnerships. While Haaland's estimated $100 million net worth comes from his Manchester City contract, bonuses and endorsements, Johansen maintains a private lifestyle with an estimated net worth of $250,000-$300,000. The couple welcomed their first son in 2024 and continue to enjoy a family-focused life. Their combined wealth, luxury properties and growing global profile keep them among football's most prominent young couples.

title: Cowboys linked to $53M Colts star in blockbuster NFL trade proposal
content: The Dallas Cowboys have been named a potential landing spot for Indianapolis Colts star Jonathan Taylor in a proposed trade. CBS Sports' Garrett Podell suggested the All-Pro running back could be available for a third-round pick if the Colts struggle in 2026. With Taylor entering the final year of his contract and coming off another standout season, Dallas could pursue him if its rushing attack fails to meet expectations.

title: Brittany Mahomes shares heartfelt promise inspired by Patrick Mahomes
content: Brittany Mahomes says becoming a mother has made her more ambitious and intentional about pursuing her goals. She hopes her children, Sterling, Bronze, and Golden, see both her and Patrick Mahomes working hard and following their dreams. Brittany believes leading by example will teach them that success comes through determination and perseverance. She also shared that her daughter Sterling has shown growing interest in her work, making those moments even more meaningful.

title: Paraguay end Germany's 44-year World Cup streak as football giants crash out
content: Germany’s long-standing reputation as World Cup penalty shootout specialists came to an end as Paraguay defeated them 4–3 on penalties after a 1–1 draw in Boston. It was Germany’s first-ever shootout loss, ending a 44-year perfect record dating back to 1982. Despite dominance in possession, missed penalties proved decisive, while Paraguay maintained their flawless shootout record.

title: UPTET admit card 2026 released: How to download hall ticket
content: UPTET Admit Card 2026: The Uttar Pradesh Education Service Selection Commission has released the UPTET Admit Card 2026 on June 30, 2026. Candidates appearing for Paper 1 and Paper 2 can download their hall tickets using their registration number or mobile number. The examination will be conducted from July 2 to July 4 in multiple shifts. Candidates must verify all details on the admit card, carry valid identity proof, and follow the prescribed examination guidelines on the test day.

title: Ice cream seller travels from Lucknow to Delhi to stab wife to death over suspicion of affair
content: A shocking incident unfolded in Karkardooma, Delhi, where a 30-year-old woman was brutally stabbed to death by her husband while she slept. The husband, suspecting infidelity, traveled from Lucknow with a pre-purchased knife. He gained entry to his sister-in-law's home, committed the crime, and was apprehended by residents before being handed over to the police. The motive appears to be a suspected affair.

title: Top stocks to buy: Stock market recommendations for today - check list
content: Stock market recommendations: JSW Energy, Biocon, BHEL, and City Union Bank - these are the top stocks to buy recommended by Somil Mehta, Head of Retail Research at Mirae Asset Sharekhan for June 30, 2026:

title: Lost brother, found on LinkedIn: 73-YO grandma finds long-lost sibling!
content: In an extraordinary twist of fate, 73-year-old Gill Thompson reunited with her long-lost half-brother, Michael Bardsley, using the unlikeliest of tools—LinkedIn. After years of yearning for connection, their heartfelt journey unfolded through video calls, leading to Michael's touching visit to Wales, where the siblings shared an emotional embrace, rediscovering a bond woven from years of untold family stories.

title: 'Staying away will be the hardest': Tendulkar's message to Stokes after retirement
content: Sachin Tendulkar led heartfelt tributes to Ben Stokes following his retirement from international cricket. Tendulkar lauded Stokes' fearless approach, match-winning ability, and inspirational leadership, calling him one of England's finest all-rounders and captains. Stokes, who bowed out after a Test series loss, expressed contentment with his decision, citing years of physical and mental demands as the primary reason for stepping away.

title: Bengaluru physiotherapist found dead at homestay in Nandi Hills; boyfriend unconscious
content: A 26-year-old physiotherapist was discovered deceased in a locked room at a homestay near Nandi Hills, with her boyfriend found unconscious beside her. Police are investigating a suspected murder-suicide attempt. The victim's mother has filed a complaint alleging a 'love jihad' angle, leading to a murder case against the boyfriend.

title: 6 dead after gunman opens fire at youth welfare centre in custody dispute in Germany
content: Tragedy struck northern Germany Monday as a gunman killed at least six people at a youth welfare facility in Stade. All victims were employees. Officials suspect a custody dispute fueled the 'extremely violent crime committed in cold blood.' The main suspect is arrested, with two others facing police scrutiny. The incident, which left several injured, has prompted a high-pressure investigation into the motive and events.

title: Oil prices ease as investors await US-Iran talks; Brent crude falls to $72 per barrel
content: Oil prices dipped as markets weighed potential US-Iran talks in Doha against ongoing regional instability. Despite recent missile exchanges and attacks on ships, crude shipments through the Strait of Hormuz have seen an uptick. Investors are cautiously optimistic about diplomatic efforts, though tangible signs of de-escalation remain elusive. Analysts suggest Gulf oil flows are steadily recovering, potentially returning to pre-conflict levels soon.

title: Watch: Djokovic seeks wedding invite from fan; Osaka stuns with 'Kill Bill' entrance
content: Wimbledon witnessed a heartwarming moment as Novak Djokovic invited himself to a fan's wedding after a proposal on Centre Court. Meanwhile, Naomi Osaka turned heads with a striking Kill Bill-inspired kimono, a creative nod to her Japanese heritage amidst the strict dress code. Both athletes delivered memorable off-court performances alongside their on-court victories.

title: PM Modi to visit US for G20, says Sergio Gor; hints Trump likely to visit India next year
content: US Ambassador to India Sergio Gor expressed enthusiasm for Prime Minister Narendra Modi's potential return visit to the United States, highlighting President Trump's continued engagement with India. Gor is optimistic about finalizing an interim trade agreement soon, noting the complex negotiations involving thousands of items. He also indicated a likely visit by President Trump to India next year, following PM Modi's attendance at the G20 Summit in Miami.

title: 'No negotiation at any level': Iran refutes Donald Trump's claim of planned US talks in Doha
content: Iran has denied any upcoming negotiations with the US, contradicting President Trump's claims of a meeting in Qatar.  While both nations reportedly agreed to halt counter-strikes following recent escalations around the Strait of Hormuz, Tehran clarified that an expert delegation will visit Doha solely to discuss the implementation of a memorandum of understanding concerning frozen assets and oil exports.  Comprehensive deal talks remain contingent on these key provisions.

title: 'Everything has taken its toll': Stokes opens up on 'negative effects' of leading England
content: The England captain announced on Sunday, in the middle of the third Test against New Zealand at Trent Bridge, that he would retire from international cricket after the match, bringing the curtain down on a 15-year career.

title: 'Gun was easily accessible': 7-month-old baby shot in the head by 10-year-old boy
content: In a deeply troubling case from Missouri, a 10-year-old boy faces murder charges after a seven-month-old baby was shot and killed. Authorities arrived at a St. Louis residence where they discovered the infant, Kiyomi Parker, suffering from a gunshot wound. It has come to light that the young suspect accessed a firearm that was concealed under a mattress.

title: Sonakshi and Zaheer enjoy family dinner with Shatrughan Sinha, Poonam Sinha
content: Sonakshi Sinha and Zaheer Iqbal were recently spotted enjoying a family dinner in Mumbai, accompanied by Sonakshi's parents, veteran actor Shatrughan Sinha and Poonam Sinha, along with Zaheer's father, Iqbal Ratansi. The family's warm outing caught the attention of paparazzi, with one heartfelt moment particularly winning over fans.

title: 'Has it become a crime to have darshan of Lord Ram?': Cong alleges Ajay Rai put under house arrest
content: Congress alleged its Uttar Pradesh chief Ajay Rai was placed under house arrest to prevent a party delegation from visiting Ayodhya's Ram Temple. The party claims this is a 'cowardly action' by the BJP government amid controversy over temple fund embezzlement. Congress leaders were reportedly detained, with the party deeming such actions unconstitutional and demanding their immediate release to proceed with their visit.

title: Karnataka Sahitya Academy to chronicle lives of 59 freedom fighters jailed in Andaman and Nicobar Islands
content: The Academy has formed a committee comprising academicians and researchers, and they will be publishing a book on the lives of the freedom-fighters from Karnataka titled ‘Yaaroo Ariyada Veeraru’

title: Apple iPhone 18 Pro supplier list, parts and photos exposed in Tata data leak
content: Several of the leaked files carried Apple “confidential” watermarks and internal Apple code-names consistent with the iPhone 18 Pro generation, according to the source familiar with the matter

title: U.P. Congress chief Ajay Rai placed under house arrest in Ayodhya ahead of party delegation’s visit
content: The party had informed the local administration about the proposed delegation’s visit to take the blessings of Lord Ram

title: Now, free ride for women in Kochi Corporation’s ro-ro ferries
content: The resolution, moved by Tax Appeal standing committee chairperson K.A. Manaf, received full backing from ruling UDF councillors, while the opposition LDF and BJP councillors were neither fully supportive nor opposed it

title: Ram Temple embezzlement case concerns assets of a juristic person and minor
content: Series of judicial decisions, right from the days of the Privy Council to Supreme Court’s judgments in the Sree Padmanabhaswamy Temple and the Ramjanmabhoomi Temple cases, hold the deity as a legal entity capable of holding property.

title: Ram Temple embezzlement row: Supreme Court urged to secure digital evidence before it is ‘quietly lost’
content: During the oral mention, Mr. Goswami informed the Bench that the Supreme Court Registry had informed him that the petition would be taken up only after the summer vacation, which ends on July 12

title: Ayodhya lawyers refuse to represent Ram Temple embezzlement accused
content: Eight persons have been named as accused in an FIR over the alleged embezzlement of donations received at the Ram temple; the accused handled cash and valuables the temple received.

title: Ayodhya Temple donation theft will have long-term consequences for the Sangh Parivar
content: The sordidness of the embezzlement issue has led to questions about the integrity of those who spearheaded the movement to build the Ram Temple in place of the Babri Masjid, with the loss of a moral high ground for the BJP and the Sangh Parivar

title: Academic bats for AI as aid, not substitute, in judiciary
content: Prof. Mishra said AI-powered technologies could significantly improve legal research, document management, case administration, translation and other court-related functions

title: Rise in food poisoning cases highlights lapses in food safety | Data
content: Nearly three-fourths of all States and Union Territories tended to have low or moderate scores on the Food Safety Index

title: What India’s 12 ‘operationally deployed’ nuclear warheads really mean
content: SIPRI’s latest assessment shows the maturation of India’s second-strike capability rather than a shift away from its long-standing ‘no first use’ doctrine; the development comes amid a broader global trend of nuclear modernisation and weakening arms-control frameworks

title: What are Indian farmers’ concerns over impacts of India-U.S. BTA | Explained
content: The farmer groups are yet again at the forefront to express their anguish through demonstrations, events and protests as USTR Greer concludes his visit following meetings with Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal

title: Why has India-U.S. trade deal been delayed? | Explained
content: From India’s Russian oil purchases to legal battles in Washington, the road to India-U.S. trade deal has become increasingly uncertain, and The Hindu explains why

title: Honeymoon murder: Meghalaya HC upholds bail to prime accused Sonam Raghuvanshi
content: Police have alleged that Sonam conspired with hired assailants to kill her husband for financial gains

title: Honeymoon murder: Meghalaya court reserves order on bail plea of key accused
content: Kushwaha is the alleged boyfriend of Raghuvanshi's wife Sonam, another accused who was granted bail on Tuesday (April 28) by the district and sessions court of Meghalaya's East Khasi district

title: Meghalaya honeymoon murder case: Raja Raghuvanshi’s family demands death penalty for wife Sonam, others
content: Meghalaya Police files a 790-page chargesheet in court against the five accused who were allegedly hired to kill Raja, a businessman from Indore during his honeymoon with Sonam in the Northeastern State in May

title: Ram temple donations case: All eight accused sent to 14 days judicial custody
content: Besides, Faizabad/Ayodhya Bar Association passes a resolution that none of its members will represent the eight accused

title: Maharashtra TET paper leak points to an inter-State racket, finds investigation team
content: Authorities are examining whether provisions of the Maharashtra Control of Organised Crime Act (MCOCA) can be invoked against those found guilty

title: Congress seeks Rajnath Singh’s resignation in controversy over Operation Sindoor deaths
content: The Defence Minister has ‘insulted’ the soldiers who laid down their lives during Operation Sindoor, the Opposition party says, asking why it had taken 13 months for their names to be made public

title: Jharkhand government appoints 1,042 new teachers
content: While handing over the job letters to the newly appointed teachers, Chief Minister Hemant Soren said that if teachers discharge their duties with honesty and diligence, both society and the State would benefit. He urged teachers to adopt a sensitive and skillful approach in their interactions with children

title: 41 migrant workers from Jharkhand stranded in T.N. after ammonia leak at Tiruvallur firm
content: Tiruvallur district officials say there has been no response from the Jharkhand government despite repeated requests to facilitate the workers’ return

title: U.S., India trade deal in final steps: Sergio Gor
content: The U.S. envoy to India cited the personal equation between U.S. President Donald Trump and Prime Minister Narendra Modi which was driving the ties.

title: Sabarimala gold theft: SIT’s interim report mentions criminal conspiracy by TDB officials
content: High Court permits special investigation team to register fresh case, including against former TDB president P.S. Prashanth

title: Master your mother tongue, the ‘Coolphabets’ way
content: Vijayawada techie and his friend have developed an app to make learning languages easier for children settled outside their native State or country

title: Calcutta HC again denies urgent hearing of Abhishek Banerjee’s plea to travel abroad
content: The petition stems from a criminal case lodged against the Diamond Harbour MP by an individual Rajib Darkar over alleged derogatory remarks made against Amit Shah at an election rally

title: I&B Ministry sets up panel to boost film industry
content: Prasoon Joshi will chair this Group; Ministry has also prepared model State Cinema Regulations for adoption by States

title: Surveillance boost: Army to raise ‘Baaz Battalions’
content: The new battalions will be built upon the Army’s existing RPA Flights and will comprise a specialist pool of trained personnel responsible for operating and managing Remotely Piloted Aircraft systems

title: JKLF chief Yasin Malik among five named in charge sheet in 1990 Sarla Bhat killing case
content: The State Investigation Agency revealed the involvement of Malik, then JKLF chief commander; Khurshid Ahmad Chalkoo; Abdul Hamid Sheikh; Mohammad Yousuf Sofi alias Idrees and Ghulam Mohammad Taploo ‘in planning and executing the abduction and brutal killing’

title: CBSE relaxes three-language policy for Class 7, 8, 9; allows two foreign language options
content: The current batch of Class 10 will not have to follow the new language policy, the education board stated

title: Bengal passes preventive detention, legal curbs Bill
content: Govt. can detain a person to prevent anti-social activity for a year; CM Suvendu Adhikari says State “will not misuse (law)... for political vendetta”; law won’t stand legal scrutiny, says Trinamool MLA

title: Telangana HC asks ECI to consider Urdu forms in SIR exercise
content: The court was hearing a petition filed by a social worker from Karimnagar district questioning the supply of enumeration forms printed exclusively in Telugu

title: Resignation of M.R. Vijayabhaskar illustrates fragile nature of truce between the rebels in AIADMK and Palaniswami
content: Mr. Vijayabhaskar, who held the portfolio of Transport during 2016-21, when Jayalalithaa, OPS, and EPS were Chief Ministers, was among the 25 dissidents who sided with the TVK-led government during the motion on vote of confidence moved by Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay in the Assembly on May 13, defying their party’s whip.

title: Canara Bank hands over ₹2,397 crore dividend cheque to FM Nirmala Sitharaman
content: The bank reported a record net profit of ₹19,187 crore in 2025-26, compared to ₹17,027 crore in the previous year, a 12.69% year-on-year increase. Government of India holds 62.93% of stake in the bank

title: As SIR begins on June 30, Karnataka govt. issues guidelines for permanent residence certificates
content: The applications will be processed by Atal Janasnehi Centres, BengaluruOne, KarnatakaOne and Grama One among others

title: State government to revisit minimum wages notified for 84 scheduled employment?
content: The Labour Department has been asked to prepare a Cabinet note, the contention being that revised wages were notified without Cabinet approval

title: Misrepresentation of facts by prosecutor led  to bail for accused in officials’ assault case: ED
content: State government had removed Geena Kumari, who was appointed during the term of the previous LDF government, from the post following the controversy

title: Madras High Court stays DVAC Inspector’s suspension
content: Justice P.T. Asha grants the stay till July 9 after arriving at a prima facie satisfaction that the suspension order was motivated

title: Udhayanidhi Stalin hits out at TVK, terms MLA resignations ‘a betrayal of the people’
content: He charges that the recent resignation of M.R. Vijayabhaskar clearly exposes the extreme levels to which the ruling party’s ‘poaching politics’ have escalated

title: Bihar Governor Hasnain and Minister of State Margherita likely to represent India at Khamenei’s funeral
content: Commentators describe the funeral in Tehran as a “big opportunity” for India to restructure its West Asia policy; they say the choice of the delegation indicate that India has not recognised the importance of the event

title: KSTA, AKSTU to hold protests on June 30
content: Left-affiliated unions to seek protection of public schools, duty off for teachers deployed for Census, and organise a convention in protest against PM SHRI scheme

title: Kerala to launch campaign in schools against epidemics
content: Aim is to make students aware of epidemic prevention measures, including the importance of cleanliness and personal hygiene practices

title: Panel will be set up to bring land laws up to date: Kerala Revenue Minister
content: The government will frame a legislation whereby marginalised sections who did not gain much from the first land reforms will benefit, says A.P. Anil Kumar

title: 'Direct threat to regional peace': India strongly condemns Pakistan air strikes on Afghan territory
content: The MEA, in its statement, reiterates its "unwavering support" for Afghanistan’s sovereignty and territorial integrity

title: 'India's Engineering Talent Give It AI Advantage Over China': Top US Official Tells NDTV
content: Asked how Washington and New Delhi could work together amid growing strategic competition with China, Helberg said the focus should be on building a positive technology partnership rather than simply competing with Beijing.

title: 15 Injured In Fire In Naphtha-Carrying Pipeline At Bengal Petrochemical Unit
content: The incident occurred between 4:00 am and 5:00 am, when flames suddenly erupted from the pipeline. Some of the injured are reported to be in critical condition.

title: Stock Market LIVE Updates, Sensex Today: Sensex Drops 250 Points Amid Mixed Global Cues
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty LIVE Updates: Brent August crude futures were down 1.03 per cent at $72.40 a barrel.

title: India-US Trade Deal In Final 1-2% Stretch, Says Envoy Sergio Gor
content: US Ambassador to India Sergio Gor said negotiators from both countries were in the final stages of concluding the long-awaited bilateral trade agreement.

title: The Real Cost Of Glass Skin: Inside India's K-Beauty Obsession
content: Dermats say it's an economic irony that Indian consumers pay premium prices for Korean beauty products that don't work in India's tropical climate.

title: 'We'll Cut Off Hands That...': Pakistan's Unprovoked Threat Over Indus Waters
content: New Delhi had placed the Indus Waters Treaty in abeyance following a deadly cross-border terrorist strike in Jammu and Kashmir's Pahalgam last year that killed 26 civilians.

title: The Morning The Sky Exploded And The Earth Was Never Quite The Same
content: Around 7:15 am on June 30 in 1908, during the reign of Tsar Nicholas II, an object from space entered Earth's atmosphere over Siberia. And it changed science forever.

content: Paul Regan, a US man, is accused of orchestrating a Ponzi scheme that raised more than $60 million from over 300 investors

content: Weather Today, Delhi-NCR Rain Alert, IMD Monsoon Update Live: Delhi sees warmest summer nights in four years with monsoon onset delayed to July.

title: Rs 5,000 SIP vs Rs 5 Lakh Fixed Deposit: Which Delivers Bigger Returns
content: Monthly SIP contributions have remained above Rs 30,000 crore through 2026, marking an increase of nearly 16 per cent compared with the previous year.

title: "Blatant Act Of Aggression": India Slams Pak For Strikes In Afghanistan
content: Pakistan launched its deadliest attack on Afghanistan in months, with Islamabad saying on Monday it killed dozens of militants as the Afghan government reported civilian casualties.

content: The shift marks a notable change in Apple's longstanding practice of packaging security fixes with broader software releases

title: Glacier Melt Triggers Flash Flood In Himachal, Over 50 Vehicles Stranded
content: According to the officials, the incident was caused by a sudden increase in glacier meltwater, even though no rainfall was reported in the area.

title: US, India Trade Deal In Final Steps: US Envoy Sergio Gor
content: Addressing the US-India Strategic Partnership Forum Leadership Summit here, Gor said he was determined to conclude the agreement as it would be beneficial to both nations.

title: Marco Rubio Plans India Visit For Second Time This Year: US Envoy
content: Asserting that American corporate confidence in New Delhi has reached a historic high, United States Ambassador to India Sergio Gor declared that the US government holds absolute confidence in India as a trusted, predictable strategic partner.

title: Iran Says No US Talks Scheduled In Qatar, Final Negotiations Yet To Begin
content: Iran has said that there are no negotiation meetings scheduled with the United States "in the coming days", dismissing speculation that its delegation's visit to Qatar is linked to any discussions with US officials.

title: Quad Meeting To Be Held In Philippines In Two Weeks: US Envoy Sergio Gor
content: Ambassador Gor confirmed that all four core partner nations, the United States, India, Australia, and Japan, are locked in and moving with absolute synchronisation to deepen their theatre-level alliances.

title: Union minister Pabitra Margherita, Bihar governor to go for Khamenei’s state funeral
content: senior foreign minister Pabitra Margherita likely to represent india at funeral of late Iranian suprem leader Ali Khamenei . govt of india will participate in the funeral of hosseini's successor mojtaba . modi is scheduled to depart for a three-nation tour of Indonesia, australia and new Zealand on July 6 .

title: 'Historic': Trump hails US SC order granting presidential authority sought since 1930s
content: At TOI World Desk, our dedicated team of seasoned journalists and passionate writers tirelessly sifts through the vast tapestry of global events to bring you the latest news and diverse perspectives round the clock. title: Women's quota bill back on table? NDA edges closer to two-thirds mark in Rajya Sabha
content: the numbers in the RS have become more favourable towards BJP-led NDA . it now has 141 MPs in the 242-member RS and can count on the support of 10 nominated and Independent MPs, taking its assured tally to 151 . if govt makes a fresh push for passage of its ambitious constitution amendment bill to increase the strength of the Lok Sabha .

title: Pakistan back in FATF Grey List? India to cite Operation Sindoor evidence in fresh push
content: Pakistan was removed from the ‘Grey List’ in Oct 2022 after it managed to satisfy members of FATF about implementation of a 34-point action plan . countries face increased FATF monitoring and are subjected to periodic review . such countries also face difficulties in accessing funds from global financial institutions .

title: 'No Chinese incursions in Arunachal': Army denies reports of PLA occupation along LAC
content: the Nah tribal community in Arunachal had raised concerns over alleged incursions . the people’s liberation Army (PLA) has occupied large parts of their traditional grazing, hunting and agricultural areas over the past six years . a memorandum to the deputy commissioner alleges the PLA’s occupation .

title: SIA charges Yasin Malik, 4 others in Kashmiri Pandit nurse Sarla Bhat's murder
content: aides named in abduction, torture and killing of 27-year-old SRINAGAR . SIA filed 737-page chargesheet for kidnap-murder . three of malik's co-accused died before the case could go to trial .

title: Entrance exams to rely less on coaching? Draft panel proposes law to regulate sector
content: Manash Pratim Gohain is a seasoned journalist with over two decades at The Times of India, where he has built a rich body of work spanning education policy, politics, and governance. Renowned for his incisive coverage of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, accreditation reforms, and skilling initiatives, he has also reported on student politics, urban policy, and social movements. His political reportage—both reflective and news-driven—adds depth to his writing, bridging policy with public impact. Through his 2,500 articles and related outlets, he has emerged as a trusted voice in national discourse, particularly in linking education reform to broader societal change. Read More

title: 42% rain deficit: This June is set to be India’s 3rd driest in 100 years
content: the average rainfall recorded across the country in June so far stands at 92.2mm, against a normal of 157.7mm . in the past 100 years only two years have seen lower overall rainfall in June — 2009 (87.5mm) and 2014 (92.1mm), both within the past 20 years . the first week of July is expected to bring better and well-distributed rains in most parts of the country .

title: 'Will cut off those hands': Pakistan minister issues threat to India over Indus Waters Treaty
content: At TOI World Desk, our dedicated team of seasoned journalists and passionate writers tirelessly sifts through the vast tapestry of global events to bring you the latest news and diverse perspectives round the clock. With an unwavering commitment to accuracy, depth, and timeliness, we strive to keep you informed about the ever-evolving world, delivering a nuanced understanding of international affairs to our readers. Join us on a journey across continents as we unravel the stories that shape our interconnected world. Read More

title: Bangladesh vows to push Teesta plan 'at any cost'; China says project not aimed at '3rd party'
content: the 'national priority' Teesta barrage master plan will be implemented at "any cost" . he was assured by china of its unwavering support, "no matter how the world changes" china-bangladesh cooperation does not target any third party, he said .
